summary: Halloween‑charged set with double‑time Big River and soaring Lazy Lightning
blurb: The first set dazzles with a blistering double‑time Big River, a soaring Lazy Lightning and tight Shakedown Street. The second half offers Space and Morning Dew but is hampered by a loose drum groove on Superstitious. Jerry’s lyrical reading on Ship of Fools adds a memorable political touch.
review:
On Halloween night 1984 the Dead delivered a festive, if uneven, performance at Berkeley Community Theater. Set one bursts open with a double‑time romp on "Big River" and a high‑octane "Lazy Lightning", while "Shakedown Street" rolls into a solid jam that captures the band’s late‑’80s groove. Jerry Garcia shines throughout, from his joyous lead lines to a politically tinged reading of "Ship Of Fools" that fans still quote. The second set trades the early fire for a more spacious vibe – "Space" and "Morning Dew" are highlights, but the drum duo stumble on "I Ain't Superstitous", even dropping the traditional Drums segment, which dulls the momentum. Bob Weir’s white sequined shirt and tie‑dye/bunny‑ears ensemble add classic Halloween flair. Overall the show is a fun, seasonal snapshot with several standout moments, though the rhythm section’s slip keeps it from reaching legendary status.

For listeners seeking the most complete experience, the Senn “gd84-10-31.senn.14947.sbeok.shnf” capture offers the full setlist with decent sound and captures the Halloween atmosphere despite the drum issues. If you only want a taste of Jerry’s exuberant playing, the excellent single‑track SBD by Walker‑Scotton is worth a spin, but it omits most of the show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Mixed sentiment dominates (three mixed, one positive). No consistent negative criticism, but only a single clear praise, so a modest positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Full‑show SBD with decent audio that captures the Halloween vibe and all setlist highlights, despite some drum shortcomings.

key_highlights: Double‑time "Big River" and soaring "Lazy Lightning" open a festive first set.
"Shakedown Street" jam is tight and energetic.
"Space" and "Morning Dew" provide strong second‑set moments.
Jerry's lyrical reading on "Ship Of Fools" adds political edge.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a double‑time lead on "Big River", joyous playing throughout, and a memorable lyrical reading on "Ship Of Fools"; his final notes are highlighted as evidence of pure enjoyment.
band_performance.Bob: Adds visual flair with a white sequined shirt and tie‑dye/bunny‑ears ensemble, contributing solid rhythm guitar work.
band_performance.Mickey: Struggles on "I Ain't Superstitous", never fully locks in; the traditional Drums segment is missing, reducing momentum.
band_performance.Billy: Shares the drum trouble on "Superstitious" with Mickey, resulting in a loose groove and absent Drums section.
